decision_policy: Repo-local decisions in dev/sm120_promotion_decisions.json mark refreshed or structurally non-promotable rows as inactive without deleting their evidence.

promotion_candidates.decision_decision: Rejected for trainer promotion; focused uncontended timing showed CUDA was faster than Triton for the GPT-2 MLP GELU-forward row.
decision_evidence: 2026-05-21 full stack refresh selected Triton by a small edge: Triton 531.483 us; CUDA 535.954 us
2026-05-21 focused uncontended refresh: CUDA 528.159 us; Triton 529.627 us
promotion_candidates.decision_note: Use as a Triton comparison row until a trainer-callable integration beats the current provider.
promotion_candidates.promotion_gate: refresh same-session baseline, add a trainer-callable Triton/C++ route, then run route parity and TinyStories

promotion_candidates.decision_decision: Rejected for trainer promotion; focused same-session source-default CUDA vec2 timing beat Triton for the wide GPT-2 bias-add shape, and a wider vec4 CUDA candidate was slower.
decision_evidence: 2026-05-21 materialized attention stack round: Triton bias_add 527.930 us versus 3-sample CUDA 528.993 us
2026-05-21 same-session CUDA 7-repeat refresh: CUDA bias_add 529.032 us
2026-05-21 focused same-session refresh: source-default CUDA vec2 measured 529.464 us versus Triton 530.461 us for BT=65536 OC=3072
2026-05-21 vec4 CUDA candidate measured 538.793 us and was rejected; its 3-step TinyStories smoke passed at avg_ms=2487.089 but the focused runtime row did not justify keeping the source change
promotion_candidates.decision_note: Use as a Triton comparison row until a trainer-callable integration beats the current provider.
promotion_candidates.promotion_gate: refresh same-session baseline, add a trainer-callable Triton/C++ route, then run route parity and TinyStories

promotion_candidates.decision_decision: Rejected for trainer promotion; Torch fp32-state AdamW is materially slower than the CUDA trainer route.
decision_evidence: Python-stack round: CUDA AdamW no-master 1926.957 us; Torch fp32-state AdamW 7449.536 us
promotion_candidates.decision_note: Use Torch for Python-side operator comparisons; trainer promotion needs a matching C++ call path and smoke gate.
promotion_candidates.promotion_gate: refresh same-session baseline, add an explicit libtorch/C++ or equivalent native route, then run route parity and TinyStories

promotion_candidates.decision_decision: Keep as profiler/runtime evidence only. The refreshed LibTorch row is the fastest observed logits-copy row, but the current GPT-2 trainer has no logits-sized device-to-device copy call-site to promote.
decision_evidence: 2026-05-21 raw-pointer LibTorch refresh: cached from_blob full-row parity PASS, CUDA runtime 8799.168 us, native CUDA kernel 8881.951 us, Torch C++ raw 8686.496 us
2026-05-21 correctness-enabled optional refresh: Torch C++ 8669.632 us versus Python Torch 8707.392 us and CUDA runtime 8778.042 us
2026-05-21 trainer source search: train_gpt2.cu has host/device copies for inputs, targets, mean loss, and sampling logits, but no logits_elems=3296722944 device-to-device copy call-site
promotion_candidates.decision_note: Keep as benchmark evidence only; no current trainer call-site maps to this row.
promotion_candidates.promotion_gate: none; profiler-only runtime row with no current trainer call-site

promotion_candidates.decision_decision: Do not promote the LibTorch gradients-zero trainer route by default. The opt-in C++ call-site now exists and passes correctness plus TinyStories smoke, but its x10 stability round regressed versus the current native x10 trainer selection.
decision_evidence: 2026-05-21 refreshed native/optional runtime logs: CUDA runtime 148.470 us, Python Torch 148.104 us, Torch C++ 147.749 us for grad_elems=124475904

2026-05-21 LibTorch runtime log scratch/sm120_rounds/codex_sm120_round_optional_refresh_correctness_20260521/bench_sm120_libtorch_runtime.log: cached from_blob full-row parity PASS for cuda_memset grad_elems=124475904

2026-05-21 standalone trainer-link probe scratch/sm120_rounds/libtorch_trainer_link_20260521/validate_libtorch_trainer_link.log: LibTorch executable without torch_python compile PASS, runtime PASS, and zero/copy from_blob probe PASS
2026-05-21 opt-in trainer route scratch/sm120_rounds/codex_sm120_round_libtorch_grad_zero_20260521/train_gpt2cu.log: grad_zero_backend Torch C++, validation passed, avg_ms=2487.730 over 3 steps

promotion_candidates.decision_note: Opt-in LibTorch trainer route passed correctness/smoke but x10 stability rejected promotion.
promotion_candidates.promotion_gate: none; opt-in trainer route passed but x10 stability rejected promotion

promotion_candidates.correctness_evidence_note: No dedicated route-level correctness log is required for this runtime primitive; the timing log and CUDA runtime API checks are the available evidence.

promotion_candidates.decision_decision: Native Torch SDPA wins only for already-separated Q/K/V; trainer-shaped TorchPacked was slower than packed TK.
decision_evidence: 2026-05-20 target-context refresh: packed TK forward 787.859 us; TorchPacked forward 1120.509 us; native Torch separated forward 570.848 us
2026-05-21 materialized packed refresh: packed TK forward 778.909 us; TorchPacked forward 1068.491 us; TorchMaterializedPacked forward 1260.536 us; native Torch separated forward 555.498 us
promotion_candidates.decision_note: Use Torch SDPA for already-separated Q/K/V experiments; packed trainer selection must compare TorchPacked.
promotion_candidates.promotion_gate: refresh same-session baseline, add a packed-QKV or separated-Q/K/V trainer path, then compare against TK and run TinyStories

promotion_candidates.decision_decision: Native Torch SDPA wins only for already-separated Q/K/V; trainer-shaped TorchPacked was slower than packed TK.
decision_evidence: 2026-05-20 target-context refresh: packed TK backward 2716.901 us; TorchPacked backward 4107.011 us; native Torch separated backward 2227.869 us
2026-05-21 materialized packed refresh: packed TK backward 2728.503 us; TorchPacked backward 4067.501 us; TorchMaterializedPacked backward 4195.885 us; native Torch separated backward 2195.661 us
promotion_candidates.decision_note: Use Torch SDPA for already-separated Q/K/V experiments; packed trainer selection must compare TorchPacked.
promotion_candidates.promotion_gate: refresh same-session baseline, add a packed-QKV or separated-Q/K/V trainer path, then compare against TK and run TinyStories

promotion_candidates.decision_decision: Do not promote the dInput-only Torch row. The full Torch native backward with dweight/dbias is slower than CUDA, the full Triton atomic prototype is also slower plus has an FP32 gradient-buffer contract mismatch, and the focused Torch dInput-plus-BF16-grads hybrid is much slower than the CUDA full-backward baseline.
decision_evidence: 2026-05-20 target-context refresh: dInput-only Torch native 217.568 us; Triton dInput-only 228.320 us
2026-05-20 target-context refresh: full Torch native backward 416.224 us; Triton atomic FP32-grad backward 363.680 us; CUDA full backward remains about 290 us
2026-05-21 focused target-context hybrid: Torch BackwardDInputNativePlusGrads 1964.480 us with BF16 dweight/dbias
promotion_candidates.decision_note: Torch native dInput-only output-mask row is useful for backward decomposition work but needs dweight/dbias before trainer promotion.
promotion_candidates.promotion_gate: add dweight/dbias accumulation before considering trainer promotion

promotion_candidates.decision_decision: Not an active trainer-promotion target for GPT-2 124M.
decision_evidence: layernorm/backward_dinput N=65536 C=3072 is outside the current GPT-2 124M trainer objective rows.
promotion_candidates.decision_note: Triton dInput-only is useful for backward decomposition work but needs dweight/dbias before trainer promotion.
promotion_candidates.promotion_gate: non-trainer GPT-2 LayerNorm shape; keep as operator evidence unless the trainer adds this shape

promotion_candidates.decision_decision: Not a GPT-2 124M trainer LayerNorm width; keep as operator stress evidence.
decision_evidence: 2026-05-20 target-context refresh: GPT-2 trainer LayerNorm width is C=768
promotion_candidates.decision_note: Native Torch is useful where saved mean/rstd are not needed; trainer LayerNorm still needs stats-compatible state.
promotion_candidates.promotion_gate: non-trainer GPT-2 LayerNorm shape; keep as operator evidence unless the trainer adds this shape

promotion_candidates.decision_decision: Not active until the candidate matches the trainer state contract.
decision_evidence: runtime/adamw_update_bf16_state params=124475904 no-master has use scope 'non-equivalent BF16-state reference', which does not match the current trainer state contract.
promotion_candidates.decision_note: Torch fused AdamW is usable only for BF16 moment-state experiments; the trainer default uses FP32 moment buffers.
promotion_candidates.promotion_gate: match the trainer optimizer state contract before considering promotion

promotion_policy: Rows are selected benchmark winners that do not yet have a trainer call path. They are prioritized by speedup over the next observed stack when that comparison exists. Native/direct and codegen rows are listed before library integrations, layout rewrites, and reference/state gaps. Before implementation, rerun the selected row and current baseline in the same session.
